title: SAS Viya Infrastructure Sizing Questionnaire
description: Ask these questions to determine the right Viya infrastructure tier for a customer's production deployment. Score each answer, sum the weighted scores, and map to the recommended tier.

scoringGuide.outputTemplate: Based on your answers, we recommend the **{tier}** profile.

questions.question: What is the total size of the datasets your analytics platform will process regularly?
questions.context: Include raw data, staging, and processed outputs. Consider both current state and data already planned for onboarding.

answers.label: Under 1 TB
answers.description: Single data source or small departmental dataset

answers.label: 1 - 10 TB
answers.description: Multiple data sources across a department or function

answers.label: 10 - 50 TB
answers.description: Cross-functional data from multiple systems enterprise-wide

answers.label: 50+ TB
answers.description: Massive data volumes — transaction logs, CDRs, national registries

questions.question: How many users will actively access the platform concurrently during peak hours?
questions.context: Count data scientists, analysts, business users viewing dashboards, and automated service accounts. Focus on peak concurrent, not total named users.

answers.label: Under 50
answers.description: Small analytics team or single business unit
Department-level with multiple analyst teams and business consumers
Multiple departments accessing shared platform
Organization-wide plus external consumers or multi-tenant

questions.question: If you need real-time or near-real-time scoring, what is your daily transaction volume?
questions.context: This applies to fraud scoring, credit decisioning, real-time offers, IoT events, or any use case requiring per-event scoring. If batch-only, select the first option.

answers.label: Batch only or <1M/day
answers.description: No real-time requirement, or very low-volume scoring

answers.label: 1 - 10M transactions/day
answers.description: Standard production scoring for a single channel or product

answers.label: 10 - 100M transactions/day
answers.description: Multi-channel scoring across multiple products or use cases

answers.label: 100M+ transactions/day
answers.description: National payment switch, telco CDR volume, or massive IoT

questions.question: How many analytical models do you expect to have deployed in production within the first 12 months?
questions.context: Include ML models, scoring rules, decision flows, and automated forecasting models. Count each unique model version actively scoring.

answers.label: 1 - 5 models
answers.description: One primary use case with a few model variants

answers.label: 5 - 20 models
answers.description: Multiple related models across a department (e.g., fraud + credit)

answers.label: 20 - 100 models
answers.description: Diverse model portfolio across business units with champion/challenger

answers.label: 100+ models
answers.description: Large-scale model factory with automated retraining and deployment

questions.question: What types of processing workloads will the platform handle?
questions.context: This determines compute architecture requirements — batch needs scheduled capacity, real-time needs always-on low-latency, streaming needs event processing infrastructure.

answers.label: Batch only
answers.description: Scheduled jobs, overnight processing, periodic model retraining

answers.label: Batch + near-real-time
answers.description: Scheduled batch plus synchronous API scoring (response <1 second)

answers.label: Batch + real-time + some streaming
answers.description: Mixed workloads including sub-second scoring and event-driven triggers

answers.label: Full spectrum: batch + real-time + streaming + complex event processing
answers.description: Continuous streaming analytics, ESP, real-time decisioning at scale

questions.question: What is the maximum acceptable unplanned downtime per month for this platform?
questions.context: This drives HA/DR architecture decisions — clustering, multi-AZ, active-active, geo-replication. Higher availability = more infrastructure cost.

answers.label: Up to 4 hours/month (99.5% uptime)
answers.description: Acceptable for non-critical analytics with planned maintenance windows

answers.label: Under 1 hour/month (99.9% uptime)
answers.description: Important production system with failover capability

answers.label: Under 15 minutes/month (99.97% uptime)
answers.description: Business-critical — revenue or compliance impact on outage

answers.label: Near-zero tolerance (99.99%+ uptime)
answers.description: Mission-critical — national infrastructure, payment processing, regulatory

questions.question: How many distinct analytics use cases or business units will the platform serve?
questions.context: This impacts multi-tenancy, resource isolation, governance complexity, and the breadth of Viya capabilities needed.

answers.label: 1 use case, 1 team
answers.description: Single focused project — e.g., one fraud model or one forecasting initiative

answers.label: 2-4 use cases, 1-2 business units
answers.description: A department with several analytics projects sharing infrastructure

answers.label: 5-10 use cases, 3-5 business units
answers.description: Cross-organizational platform shared by risk, marketing, operations, etc.

answers.label: 10+ use cases, 5+ BUs or external tenants
answers.description: Enterprise-wide or multi-entity platform with tenant isolation

questions.question: What is your expected growth in data volume and user count over the next 12 months?
questions.context: Size for where you'll be in a year, not just today. Under-sizing leads to costly re-architecture. Cloud deployments can flex more easily than on-prem.

answers.label: Stable — minimal growth expected
answers.description: Focused deployment with well-defined, bounded scope

answers.label: Moderate — 25-50% growth in data/users
answers.description: Adding new data sources or onboarding adjacent teams

answers.label: Significant — 2-3x growth in data/users
answers.description: Expanding to new BUs, new geographies, or adding real-time workloads

answers.label: Rapid — 3x+ growth, new channels, or regulatory mandates driving expansion
answers.description: National rollout, new digital channels, or compliance deadlines forcing scale-up

scoringLogic.calculation: For each question, multiply the selected answer's score by the question's weight. Sum all weighted scores.

example.scenario: A mid-size bank deploying fraud + credit scoring
example.recommendation: Based on your answers, we recommend the **Professional** profile.

notes: If any single answer scores at 'National Scale' level for transaction volume (q3) or availability (q6), consider bumping the overall recommendation up one tier regardless of total score.
Growth projection (q8) is weighted lowest because cloud deployments can scale — but flag it as a risk factor for on-prem customers.
When the total falls on a tier boundary, recommend the higher tier if the customer has regulatory compliance drivers.
